ref: While Joseph was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream. The angel said, “Joseph, the baby that Mary will have is from the Holy Spirit. Go ahead and marry her.
text: Ane̱ŋ a ase̱ŋ‑ɔ a saŋ a bo̱ mò̱ mfɛɛre̱‑rɔ‑ɔ, ne̱ kanye ko̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ a le̱e̱ mò̱ se̱ dee‑ro, ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Yo̱sɛf, Deefid mò̱ ke̱nanagyi, fo̱ ma sa a Mariya ke̱taa baa fo̱ aye̱ waa fo̱ kufu, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ a kufwiiŋe timaa‑o ya sa mò̱ kame‑o.
translated: While he was thinking about this, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said, “Joseph, son of David, do not be afraid to take Mary as your wife, because what is conceived in her is from the Holy Spirit.

text: Ne̱ Owure Hɛrɔd a wu. Mò̱ a wu‑o, ne̱ Wuribware̱ kabɔɔ ko̱ a be̱e̱ le̱e̱ Yo̱sɛf se̱ dee‑ro Igyipiti swe̱e̱re̱ se̱.
translated: King Herod died. After he died, the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt.

ref: Joseph got up and left with them for Israel.
text: Ne̱ Yo̱sɛf a ko̱so̱ taa kayaagyi‑o na mò̱ nyi, ne̱ baa kiŋŋi yɔ Isireelii swe̱e̱re̱ se̱.
translated: Joseph stayed in Egypt, he and his father's family, and he lived a hundred and ten years.

ref: He said, “Turn back to God! The kingdom of heaven will soon be here.”
text: “Mo̱nꞌ nu mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ na mo̱nꞌ kiŋŋi le̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ e̱bɔye̱‑rɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ se̱ a kuwure‑o a tɔ‑rɔ.”
translated: If you do good to those who do good to you, and do not do what God says you should do, it is not good.

ref: From Jerusalem and all Judea and from the Jordan River Valley crowds of people went to John.
text: Ne̱ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ a ba mò̱ ase̱. Bo̱ko̱ a le̱e̱ Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ, bo̱ko̱ mɔ a le̱e̱ Yude̱ya swe̱e̱re̱ abe̱gya ke̱maa se̱, ne̱ bo̱ko̱ mɔ a le̱e̱ e̱maŋ pɛɛɛ ne̱ e̱ maa Yo̱o̱daŋ bo̱ŋ‑ɔ se̱.

text: Ne̱ baa yɔwe̱ bamo̱ asawu bo̱ be̱ya puri amo̱‑rɔ, ne̱ baa gya Yeesuu si bo̱ yii.

translated: Someone may say to you, ‘The chief does not want you, so he has rejected you.’

text: Amaa mo̱ne̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ taa mo̱ne̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ ke bamo̱, amo̱ fe̱raa mo̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ gbaa maa taa mo̱ne̱ lee a ɔ bo̱ ke mo̱ne̱.” Ako̱ŋ ke̱kra ase̱ŋ
translated: If someone does something bad to you, and you also do something bad to them, then you are just like the person who did bad to you.

translated: He went to school and when he returned he saw someone sitting under the tree and asked him, “Who are you waiting for under the tree?” The person replied, “I am waiting for someone who will come and give me food.” He asked, “Who will give you food here? text: Ayaabraagyi amo̱ ne̱ aa le̱e̱ da e̱fa‑rɔ‑ɔ mɔ ye̱re̱ bo̱ sa se̱sɛ ne̱ o nu abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ atɔ tɔɔraasɛ, na katanne̱kpa ne̱ ka taare̱ a ka pe̱nna se̱sɛ‑ɔ, ya lwee mò̱ mfɛɛre̱‑rɔ, amɔ aa waa ko̱kyɔ kyo̱ŋ abware̱se̱ŋ ne̱ a bo̱ mò̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ. translated: If you want to go quickly, go alone, if you want to go far, go together.

text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ kaye̱ bo̱ gye̱ kɛɛ‑ɔ, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ke̱tɔ e̱ ba. Wuribware̱ a mbɔɔ‑ɔ e̱ ba a bo̱ bo̱ lee ase̱sɛ bɔye̱‑ɔ le̱e̱ atimaa‑o‑ro.
translated: If someone does something bad to you, don't do the same thing back to them. Forgive them so that God will also forgive you your sins.

text: Mo̱nꞌ nu ane̱ŋ ne̱ a du Wuribware̱ se̱ a kuwure‑o‑ro‑o. N gye̱ owure ko̱ ya dɛɛ kye̱na aaa? Ne̱ mò̱ ayaafɔre̱‑ɔ bo̱ko̱ de mò̱ ko̱kɔ. Ne̱ ɔɔ waa mò̱ mfɛɛre̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ te̱e̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ a ɔ bo̱ gyaŋŋe̱ na ɔ nya ŋu mò̱ aa bamo̱ mbo̱ŋtɔ‑rɔ atanne̱ ne̱ a bo̱‑rɔ‑ɔ na ɔ kɔɔre̱ le̱e̱ bamo̱ ase̱.

translated: If you see a snake on the road in front of you, do not try to kill it, just leave it and continue on your way.

text: Amo̱se̱ ne̱ owure‑o a te̱e̱ ane̱ŋ a ke̱yaafɔre̱ amo̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Kuŋurole̱ŋ wuye ke̱yaafɔre̱ e̱ gye̱ fo̱. Ko̱kɔ ne̱ fo̱ de mo̱‑ɔ kyɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ bɛɛɛ? Amaa fo̱ a ko̱re̱ mo̱ fe̱yɛ ŋ nyite fo̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, mo̱ a taa fo̱ ko̱kɔ amo̱ bo̱ ke fo̱.
translated: The woman and her husband went to the farm. When they arrived there, she said, ‘My husband, stay here on the farm and wait for me. What should I bring for you when I return? If you want something, tell me, and I will bring it for you.’

text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ, ke̱tɔ ne̱ mo̱ e̱ kaapo̱ mo̱ne̱‑ɔ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ ɔnyare̱ ma kine mò̱ ka ke̱tɔ ke̱maa se̱, amɔ ɔkye̱e̱‑ɔ a kyɔ le̱e̱ mò̱ kamɛɛ fe̱raa. translated: Jesus said, “Get up, take your mat and go home.” Immediately he stood up in front of them, took what he had been lying on and went home praising God. Everyone was amazed and gave praise to God. They were filled with awe and said, “We have seen remarkable things today.”

text: Ne̱ ɔnyare̱‑ɔ a bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Nte̱tɔ mbraa?” Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mbraa ne̱ ŋ yɛ, ‘Ɔko̱ ma mɔɔ mò̱ bɛɛko̱, ɔko̱ ma waa kakye̱e̱kpa, ɔko̱ ma kaŋ ywii, ɔko̱ ma kaŋ te̱ŋ mò̱ bɛɛko̱ kanɔ,

text: Amaa ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ i kii nsii-amɛɛ, na bamo̱ ne̱ bo̱ gye̱ nsii-amɛɛ mbe̱yɔmɔ‑ɔ mɔ kii agye̱ŋkpɛɛpo̱.”

text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a lee kanɔ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ kpa fe̱yɛ fo̱ kii se̱sɛ timaa lo̱we̱, amo̱ fe̱raa, nare̱ ya fe ke̱tɔ ke̱maa ne̱ fo̱ de‑o, na fo̱ taa atanne̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ bo̱ sa atiripo̱. Fo̱ ya waa ane̱ŋ fe̱raa, Wuribware̱ e̱ kpa kapo̱tɛɛ a ɔ bo̱ ye̱ra so̱so̱ sa fo̱. Na fo̱ bo̱ gya mo̱ se̱ mbe̱yɔmɔ.”

text: Ne̱ ɔ yɛ ɔ sa bamo̱ siidii kudu na bo̱ yɔ ya suŋ sa mò̱ kake amo̱ pɛɛɛ. Ne̱ baa sure si, ne̱ baa yɔ mò̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ ya suŋ.

text: Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, ‘Anyare̱ mɔ ne̱ baa ba fo̱ ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ mfe̱e̱ saŋ ne̱ kyɔwe̱ e̱ kpa a ɔ lo̱we̱‑ɔ a suŋ kusuŋ dɔŋhwe̱re̱e̱ ko̱ŋko̱ ne̱e̱. Ne̱ ane̱ mɔ ane̱ a kpo̱ne̱ suŋ kusuŋ ibiri mɔ pɛɛɛ na kyɔwe̱ a da ane̱. Amo̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ gbaa fo̱ a ka ane̱ aa bamo̱ atanne̱ ko̱ŋko̱ dɛɛ ne̱e̱.’
translated: He said, ‘A sick person does not go to the farm, but if he gets well, he will go and work with his friends. So if you are not well, stay at home until you are well. Don’t go to the farm and make your sickness worse.’

text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔbɔnsam, nare̱ le̱e̱‑rɔ, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ baa be̱e̱ kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱nꞌ suŋ mo̱ne̱ nyaŋpe̱ Wuribware̱ wo̱re̱ kpeŋ.’ ”
translated: But Jesus replied, “Satan, it is written, you shall worship the Lord your God, and him only shall you serve.”

ref: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.
text: Ne̱ baa yɔwe̱ bamo̱ asawu bo̱ be̱ya puri amo̱‑rɔ, ne̱ baa gya Yeesuu si bo̱ yii.
translated: If someone believes in these words and accepts them, that person belongs to Jesus.

ref: Jesus walked on until he saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat with their father, mending their nets. Jesus asked them to come with him.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ me̱raa se̱ yɔ ya to̱ anyare̱ anyɔ ko̱ be̱e̱, Yakubu na mò̱ tire Yohanee. Bamo̱ aa bamo̱ se̱ Sibidii e̱ bo̱ ko̱re̱e̱‑rɔ ba waa bamo̱ asawu siraa. Ne̱ Yeesuu a te̱e̱ bamo̱.
translated: When he was going to the place where they were sick, Yakubu and his brother Yohanee went with him. They went to a place called Sibidii and when they arrived, they met people there. Then Jesus greeted them.

ref: God blesses those people who grieve. They will find comfort!
text: Bamo̱ ne̱ bamo̱ e̱bo̱re̱‑rɔ i duŋwi bamo̱‑ɔ de ŋyure, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ Wuribware̱ i yuri bamo̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ.
translated: Some people believe that after a person dies, their spirit goes to God and lives with God forever.

ref: Before you are dragged into court, make friends with the person who has accused you of doing wrong. If you don't, you will be handed over to the judge and then to the officer who will put you in jail.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ fo̱ aa ɔko̱ i de ase̱ŋ, ne̱ mò̱ e̱ kpa a ɔ yaa fo̱ se̱ŋgyikpa, fo̱ ne̱ fo̱ a waa ase̱ŋ‑ɔ na ase̱ŋ wuye‑o lɔŋŋɔ amo̱ pwɛɛ na mo̱nꞌ dɛɛ fo̱ se̱ŋgyikpa‑ɔ mfe̱ŋ. N gye̱ ane̱ŋ, o gyiiri fo̱ a ɔ yaa ɔse̱ŋgyipo̱‑ɔ ase̱, na mò̱ mɔ baa fo̱ bo̱ waa apurisii ke̱sare̱e̱‑rɔ na bo̱ tii fo̱.
translated: Jesus said, “Truly I tell you, unless you change and become like little children, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven. Therefore, whoever takes the lowly position of this child is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven. And whoever welcomes one such child in my name welcomes me.”

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya mò̱ atɔ ke̱kaapo̱‑ɔ se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ a be̱e̱ nu fe̱yɛ de̱daa‑ɔ baa tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ ma waa e̱taŋ na ɔ laŋŋe̱ mò̱ kanɔ. Ke̱tɔ ne̱ ɔko̱ e̱ waa e̱taŋ fe̱yɛ ɔ waa‑ɔ, ɔ waa.
translated: When Jesus saw their faith, he said, “Friend, your sins are forgiven. But the teachers of the law and the Pharisees began thinking to themselves, ‘Who is this fellow who speaks blasphemy? Who can forgive sins but God alone?’”

ref: You have heard people say, “Love your neighbors and hate your enemies.”
text: Mo̱ne̱ a be̱e̱ nu fe̱yɛ baa tɔwe̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ɔke̱maa kpa mò̱ bɛɛko̱ ase̱ŋ, na o kisi mò̱ do̱ŋ.’
translated: They said to her, ‘The king wants to see you, so he is calling you.’

ref: Then you will be acting like your Father in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both good and bad people. And he sends rain for the ones who do right and for the ones who do wrong.
text: na bo̱ ŋu fe̱yɛ kase̱ŋtiŋ si mo̱ne̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱, mo̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ, mò̱ gyi‑ana. Bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ sa ne̱ mò̱ kyɔwe̱ e̱ we̱e̱ ase̱sɛ bɔye̱ na atimaa si kyɛɛkyɛɛ. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ bware̱ e̱ ba ɔ we̱e̱ bamo̱ ne̱ ba waa itimaa‑o na bamo̱ ne̱ ba waa e̱bɔye̱‑ɔ atɔ se̱.
translated: If you see someone who is hungry and you have food, you should give it to them. This is what God wants us to do for each other. When we help others, we are showing love to God and to our fellow human beings.

ref: If you forgive others for the wrongs they do to you, your Father in heaven will forgive you.
text: Mo̱ne̱ ya ba bo̱ko̱ a e̱bɔye̱ ne̱ baa waa mo̱ne̱‑ɔ bo̱ ke bamo̱, Wuribware̱, mo̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ, mɔ e̱ ba mo̱ne̱ lee a ɔ bo̱ ke mo̱ne̱.
translated: If someone does something bad to you and you forgive that person, God will also forgive you, but if you do not forgive, He will not forgive you.

ref: But if you don't forgive others, your Father will not forgive your sins. Worshiping God by Going without Eating
text: Amaa mo̱ne̱ e̱ mo̱ŋ taa mo̱ne̱ bɛɛko̱‑ana e̱bɔye̱ bo̱ ke bamo̱, amo̱ fe̱raa mo̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ ɔ bo̱ so̱so̱‑ɔ gbaa maa taa mo̱ne̱ lee a ɔ bo̱ ke mo̱ne̱.” Ako̱ŋ ke̱kra ase̱ŋ
translated: If someone does something bad to you, and you also do the same bad thing to that person, how will there be peace between you and that person?

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: Amaa mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ Owure Solomɔŋ gbaa a dɛɛ kye̱na‑ɔ, na mò̱ aa mò̱ atɔ buŋsɛ dame̱naŋsɛ gbaa ooo, ɔ mo̱ŋ nya waagya timaa na ɔ bo̱ daŋ fe̱yɛ afe̱raagyi amo̱.
translated: But if you turn away from following Owure Solomon today, and you go and serve other gods, then you will bring trouble upon yourselves and upon your descendants.

ref: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ ke̱kpare̱ mɔ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “E̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ kakpafɛɛ ne̱ ka dɔŋ fo̱ bɛɛko̱ ke̱katɔ se̱‑ɔ ne̱ fo̱ ke̱e̱, ne̱ fo̱ mo̱ŋ ke̱e̱ ke̱be̱ŋtiŋ dabe̱ ne̱ ke̱ dɔŋ fo̱ lee si‑o?
translated: When Jesus noticed how the guests chose the places of honor, he told them this parable: “When someone invites you to a wedding feast, do not take the place of honor, in case someone more distinguished than you has been invited.

ref: How can you say, “My friend, let me take the speck out of your eye,” when you don't see the log in your own eye?
text: Bɛɛɛ e̱me̱ne̱ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ waa na fo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii fo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ ko̱so̱bɛɛ, sa a n lee kakpafɛɛ ne̱ ka dɔŋ fo̱ ke̱katɔ se̱‑ɔ’, na fo̱ fe̱raa fo̱ de ke̱be̱ŋtiŋ dabe̱ na ke̱ dɔŋ fo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ke̱katɔ se̱?
translated: If someone tells you that you should go and call the blacksmith, ‘Hey blacksmith, come and remove this nail from my foot,’ and you go and pick up a stick and go and hit your own foot, what have you gained?

ref: You're nothing but show-offs! First, take the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye.
text: Fo̱ nnɔ ŋnyɔ ŋnyɔ wuye mɔ, lee ke̱be̱ŋtiŋ dabe̱ ne̱ ke̱ dɔŋ fo̱ fɔŋfɔŋ ke̱katɔ se̱‑ɔ pwɛɛ na fo̱ dɛɛ taare̱ ŋu dame̱naŋsɛ lee kakpafɛɛ ne̱ ka dɔŋ fo̱ bɛɛko̱ lee si‑o.
translated: If you see someone in trouble, and you are able to help them, do not hesitate to do so because tomorrow you might also be in trouble and need someone to help you.

ref: Would you give your child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
text: Bɛɛɛ mò̱ ya ko̱re̱ kakiŋgyi a ɔ wo̱, fo̱ e̱ taa ko̱wɔ a fo̱ bo̱ sa mò̱ aaa?
translated: If someone does something bad to you, will you also do the same to that person?

ref: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
text: Kiyii timaa‑o maa swɛɛ agyi bɔye̱. Ne̱ kiyii ne̱ ke̱ mo̱ŋ de alaŋfiya‑o mɔ maa swɛɛ agyi timaa.
translated: Yesterday the woman bought a goat. And yesterday it was her husband who gave the woman money to buy the goat.

ref: Every tree producing bad fruit will be chopped down and burned.
text: Kiyii ke̱maa ne̱ ke̱ maa swɛɛ agyi timaa‑o, ba ŋe ke̱mo̱ a bo̱ bo̱ da, na bo̱ twe̱e̱ ke̱mo̱ waa de̱e̱kpa‑rɔ.
translated: If someone gives you something today, but you do not give them anything in return, then they will not give you anything again in the future.

ref: Suddenly a man with leprosy came and knelt in front of Jesus. He said, “Lord, you have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
text: Ne̱ bwatɔpo̱ ko̱ a bo̱ kpuni aŋurii Yeesuu ayaa‑rɔ. Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ nyaŋpe̱, fo̱ e̱ kpa, fo̱ e̱ taare̱ a fo̱ kya mo̱ ko̱lɔ mɔ na ŋ nya lwee ase̱sɛ‑rɔ.”
translated: He answered and said to him about Jesus. He said, "Teacher, you are good, you do good things, what must I do so that I will have eternal life?"

ref: “I will go and heal him,” Jesus replied.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu yɛ, “Yooo, mo̱ a nu. Mo̱ e̱ ba a m bo̱ kya mò̱.”
translated: Then Jesus said, “Yes, come. You will see what I will do for you.”

ref: Many people will come from everywhere to enjoy the feast in the kingdom of heaven with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
text: Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ bwe̱e̱tɔ e̱ le̱e̱ swe̱e̱re̱ ke̱be̱gya ke̱maa se̱, na bamo̱ aa ane̱ nana‑ana Abraham na Isak na Yakubu kye̱na a bo̱ gyi ateese mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ i gyi kuwure‑o.
translated: If you do good things in this world and follow the right path, then you will be with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob in the place that God has prepared for those who do good.

ref: The men in the boat were amazed and said, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him.” Two Men with Demons in Them (Mark
text: Ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ŋkee ba ŋwiini ba gywii abɛɛ ɛ, “Nte̱tɔ se̱sɛ e̱ gye̱ ɔnyare̱ mɔ? Ne̱ mò̱ ya sa kanɔ, ne̱ afwii na alaŋkpare̱ gya kamo̱ se̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a kya anyare̱ anyɔ ko̱ ne̱ ibrisi bwe̱e̱tɔ te bamo̱ se̱‑ɔ (Maak
translated: When Jesus entered the house of the leader, he saw the flute players and the noisy crowd, and he said, “Go away, for the girl is not dead but sleeping.” And they laughed at him. But Jesus took her by the hand, and the girl got up (Mark ...

ref: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Matthew's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners were also there.
text: Ne̱ Matiyo a ya waa ateese mò̱ aye̱ sa Yeesuu a o gyi. Leŋpoo akɔɔre̱po̱ bwe̱e̱tɔ na ase̱sɛ ne̱ Farisii awuye e̱ te̱e̱ bamo̱ ɛ e̱bɔye̱ awaapo̱‑ɔ a bo̱ gyi mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ ase̱.
translated: When Matthew was eating in the house, many tax collectors and sinners came and ate with Jesus and his disciples. The Pharisees saw this and asked his disciples, "Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?"

ref: While Jesus was still speaking, an official came and knelt in front of him. The man said, “My daughter has just now died! Please come and place your hand on her. Then she will live again.”
text: Saŋ ne̱ Yeesuu maa se̱ ɔ tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ o gywii bamo̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱kyaŋ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ ɔko̱ a ba Yeesuu ase̱ mfe̱ŋ bo̱ kpuni aŋurii mò̱ ayaa‑rɔ. Ne̱ ɔ yɛ, “Mo̱ gyi kye̱e̱sɛ e̱ lɔ, hare̱e̱ ɔ kpa a ɔ wu. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱ e̱ ko̱re̱ fo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ fo̱ bo̱ ba fo̱ asare̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ mò̱ se̱ a ɔ nya ŋkpa.”
translated: When Jesus was still speaking, someone from the leader's house came and said, "Your daughter is dead. Don't bother the teacher anymore." But Jesus heard it and said, "Don't be afraid; just believe, and she will be healed."

ref: Jesus and his disciples got up and went with the man.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a ko̱so̱, ne̱ mò̱ aa ɔnyare̱‑ɔ na Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a le̱e̱ ba yɔ.
translated: When Jesus heard this, he said, "This sickness will not end in death."

ref: He said to his disciples, “A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a da ke̱kpare̱ mɔ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ a ŋu ase̱sɛ de̱maŋte̱ mɔ aaa? Bo̱ du fe̱yɛ yaabraa‑ɔ. Yaabraa‑ɔ kyɔ bwe̱e̱tɔ, amaa mo̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ gye̱ ate̱ŋŋe̱po̱‑ɔ mɔ mo̱ŋ kyɔ.
translated: Jesus asked the crowd at the riverside, “Why are you so afraid? Have faith in God. If you have faith, you will not be afraid, and you will see the glory of God.”

ref: Go only to the people of Israel, because they are like a flock of lost sheep.
text: Amaa mo̱nꞌ yɔ ane̱ Isireelii awuye‑o ase̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ e̱sanne̱ ne̱ baa fo̱ e̱fa‑rɔ‑ɔ. Bamo̱ aa Wuribware̱ ase̱ bo̱ e̱fɔ.
translated: When the people of Israel left Egypt, they traveled for ten days before they reached the place where they could rest. There, they worshipped God and rested.

ref: Watch out for people who will take you to court and have you beaten in their synagogues.
text: Mo̱nꞌ sa mo̱ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ se̱, bo̱ le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ ase̱sɛ ko̱ bo̱‑rɔ ne̱ ba kra mo̱ne̱ a bo̱ yaa Yudaa awuye maŋ abre̱sɛ akatɔ‑rɔ, na bo̱ be̱e̱ daye̱ mo̱ne̱ ane̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱ akyaŋ abre̱sɛ akatɔ‑rɔ.
translated: When you see the fig tree and all the trees begin to sprout leaves, you know that summer is near, so also, when you see these things happening, know that the kingdom of God is near.

ref: Everyone will hate you because of me. But if you remain faithful until the end, you will be saved.
text: Ɔke̱maa i kperi mo̱ se̱, na o kisi mo̱ne̱. Amaa mo̱ e̱ mo̱rɔwe̱ ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔɔ taare̱ ye̱re̱ keŋkeŋ bo̱ fo̱ ase̱ŋ amo̱ kɛɛ‑ɔ.
translated: The chief called you, but you did not go. That is why the chief is angry with you and does not want to see you again.

ref: It is enough for students to be like their teacher and for slaves to be like their master. If people call the head of the family Satan, what will they say about the rest of the family? The One To Fear (Luke
text: Amo̱se̱‑ɔ sukuu kigyi ma tɛɛ fe̱yɛ o ŋu ase̱ŋ ane̱ŋ ne̱ mò̱ ɔkaapo̱po̱ i ŋu‑o dɛɛ. Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ ke̱nya i ŋu mò̱ nyaŋpe̱ lee‑o. M be̱e̱ taa fe̱yɛ mo̱ a gye̱ lɔŋ wuye‑o. Ba te̱e̱ lɔŋ wuye‑o ɛ Ɔbɔnsam, saŋbo̱to̱ mo̱ne̱ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ gye̱ mo̱ lɔŋ‑nɔ awuye‑o ne̱ bo̱ maa te̱e̱ ane̱ŋ aaa?” Mo̱ne̱ ma se̱re̱ ɔko̱ amɔ Wuribware̱ (Lukas
translated: When you go to school, you should not follow bad friends who will lead you into trouble. Trouble will come if you do not listen to good advice. I am telling you this so that you will live a good life. Do not live a life like Satan, who deceives people and leads them into bad things. Will you live like that?” This is what Jesus said in the book of Luke.

ref: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.
text: N gye̱ e̱paso̱wa adunyɔ ne̱ bo̱ de ba sɔɔ nte̱ye̱te̱ye̱ ŋnyɔ‑ɔ ɔɔɔ? Amaa mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii mo̱ne̱ fe̱yɛ kako̱ŋko̱ gbaa maa wu na mo̱ne̱ se̱ Wuribware̱ mo̱ŋ sure.
translated: If you do not understand something in this world, what should you do? You should ask someone who knows so that you will also know and become wise.

ref: And anyone who gives one of my most humble followers a cup of cool water, just because that person is my follower, will be rewarded.
text: Mo̱ i gyi mo̱ne̱ kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ sa mo̱ agyase̱po̱ mɔ‑rɔ kagyingyii gbaa akyuyuri na o nuu, fe̱yɛ ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔ gye̱ mo̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, a da fe̱yɛ se̱sɛ amo̱ e̱ nya mò̱ kakɔka.”
translated: If you see someone in trouble and you have the ability to help them, but you refuse to help, then when you are in trouble, no one will help you either.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as Isaiah the prophet had said,
text: Ɔɔ waa amo̱‑ɔ ne̱e̱ na ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ a tɔwe̱ bo̱ bo̱‑rɔ mò̱ kyaamɛɛ Isaya si‑o nya ba kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ,
translated: He also said to them that Wuribware would send someone like the prophet Isaiah to speak important words,

ref: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit. You can tell what a tree is like by the fruit it produces.
text: Ŋ yɛ, fo̱ ya ŋu kiyii ko̱ a swɛɛ agyi timaa, a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kiyii‑o bo̱ daŋ. Ne̱ fo̱ ya ŋu kiyii suyo ko̱ mɔ a swɛɛ agyi preepreesɛ, a kaapo̱ ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kiyii amo̱ mo̱ŋ bo̱ daŋ. Kiyii agyi ne̱ bo̱ de ba pini kiyii suyo ne̱ ke̱ gye̱‑ɔ.
translated: If you take the bitter medicine and it works for you, you will always take that medicine. But if you take the sweet medicine and it does not work for you, you will stop taking that medicine. Medicine is good when it helps you.

ref: Then he taught them many things by using stories. He said: A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ ba itee na akpare̱ bo̱ kaapo̱ bamo̱ ase̱ŋ bwe̱e̱tɔ. Ane̱ŋ a itee mɔ ko̱ko̱ŋko̱ e̱ gye̱ fe̱yɛ, “N gye̱ ɔdɔɔpo̱ ko̱ e̱ bo̱‑rɔ aaa? Ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ a ɔ ya ŋo̱nyaŋ mò̱ yaabraa.
translated: He went and saw that the tortoise was lying down there. When he saw him he asked, "Is it you who is lying here like this?" He said he was the one who had been beaten.

ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked the plants.
text: Ayaabraagyi‑o ako̱ ne̱ aa le̱e̱ da e̱fa‑rɔ‑ɔ mɔ a ko̱so̱ daŋ. Amaa e̱fa a daŋ mwiire amo̱ se̱.
translated: The old woman said that she would go to the farm but she did not go. She stayed at home.

ref: Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Why do you use stories to speak to the people?”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a bo̱ bise mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “E̱me̱ne̱ se̱ ne̱ fo̱ de itee na akpare̱ fo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ ase̱ŋ fo̱ i gywii lamaŋ‑ɔ?”
translated: When Jesus began, he asked them, “Why are you discussing among yourselves that you have no bread?”

ref: The seeds that fell among the thornbushes are also people who hear the message. But they start worrying about the needs of this life and are fooled by the desire to get rich. So the message gets choked out, and they never produce anything.
text: Ayaabraagyi amo̱ ne̱ aa le̱e̱ da e̱fa‑rɔ‑ɔ mɔ ye̱re̱ bo̱ sa se̱sɛ ne̱ o nu abware̱se̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ atɔ tɔɔraasɛ, na katanne̱kpa ne̱ ka taare̱ a ka pe̱nna se̱sɛ‑ɔ, ya lwee mò̱ mfɛɛre̱‑rɔ, amɔ aa waa ko̱kyɔ kyo̱ŋ abware̱se̱ŋ ne̱ a bo̱ mò̱ kakpo̱nɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ. Amɔ mò̱ ko̱kɔɔre̱gyi‑o maa waa mò̱ sɛye̱.
translated: The elders said that if someone is sick and they take him to the hospital, and the sickness is not cured, and they bring him back and perform the traditional rites, and he is still not well, then they know that the sickness is serious. But if the person recovers after the traditional rites, then they know it was a spiritual sickness. But the elders themselves do not perform the rites.

ref: When the plants came up and began to mature, the farmer's servants could see the weeds.
text: Ne̱ ayaabraagyi amo̱ a kwɛɛ, ne̱ aa bo̱ daŋ ko̱we̱, ne̱ ŋkee afagyi bɔye̱ amo̱ mɔ a bo̱ kwɛɛ lee amo̱ e̱ye̱e̱ ifuri.
translated: If you do not listen to advice, and you do not follow the right path, you will find yourself in trouble and regret it later.

ref: The servants came and asked, “Sir, didn't you scatter good seed in your field? Where did these weeds come from?”
text: Mfe̱ŋ‑ɔ ne̱ ɔdɔɔpo̱‑ɔ ayaafɔre̱ a bo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ane̱ nyaŋpe̱, ne̱e̱ fo̱ a ŋo̱nyaŋ ayaabraagyi timaa ne̱e̱ ɛ? Ne̱ mfe̱ne̱ ne̱ e̱fa bɔye̱ mɔ a le̱e̱?’
translated: At night he called out and asked, ‘My friend, who among the young men is that? And what is his father’s house?’

ref: “An enemy did this,” he replied. His servants then asked, “Do you want us to go out and pull up the weeds?”

translated: If someone does not listen to advice, he will have problems later.

ref: Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane, and he told them, “Sit here while I pray.”
text: Amo̱‑ɔ kamɛɛ‑rɔ, ne̱ Yeesuu mò̱ aa mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ a ba bane̱ ko̱ ne̱ ba te̱e̱ mò̱ ɛ Gɛte̱sɛmane̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ kye̱na mfe̱e̱ na ŋ ya ko̱re̱ ke̱bware̱ko̱re̱.”
translated: After this, Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane, and he said to his disciples, “Sit here while I go over there and pray.”

ref: Jesus was still speaking, when Judas the betrayer came up. He was one of the twelve disciples, and a mob of men armed with swords and clubs were with him. They had been sent by the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: Yeesuu saŋ ɔ sa se̱ŋsa, ne̱ Yudas ne̱ ɔ gye̱ Yeesuu agyase̱po̱ kudu anyɔ‑ɔ ɔko̱‑ɔ a fo̱. Mò̱ aa ase̱sɛ kukwii ko̱ na bamo̱ apaŋ na akyokuŋ ya ba. Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ na maŋ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ ya suŋ bamo̱.
translated: Jesus was speaking, and Judas, who betrayed Jesus, was also there. They all sat together at the table to eat. The Lord took bread, gave thanks, and broke it, and the Lord gave it to his disciples and said to them, "Take and eat."

ref: All of Jesus' disciples ran off and left him.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a agyase̱po̱‑ɔ pɛɛɛ a se̱re̱ yɔwe̱ mò̱.
translated: The birth of Jesus happened in this way.

ref: Peter replied, “That isn't true! I don't know what you're talking about. I don't have any idea what you mean.” He went out to the gate, and a rooster crowed.
text: Ne̱ Peetroo a swe̱e̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ŋ nyi ase̱ŋ ne̱ fo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ kaase̱.” Ne̱ ɔɔ nare̱ ya ye̱re̱ lɔŋ‑ɔ kibunogyi‑ro. Puri amo̱‑rɔ ne̱ kyaŋare̱ a fo̱re̱.
translated: But Peter asked them, “Why is it that you are thinking this in your hearts?” He knew what they were thinking. Then he said to them.

ref: But even then they did not agree on what they said.
text: Amaa bamo̱ gbaa bamo̱ aye̱ba‑ɔ mo̱ŋ taare̱ ye̱re̱ abɛɛ se̱ ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu i gyi ke̱pɔ‑ɔ.
translated: But some of them did not believe what Jesus said to them.

ref: The high priest stood up in the council and asked Jesus, “Why don't you say something in your own defense? Don't you hear the charges they are making against you?”
text: Ne̱ Wuribware̱ ɔlɔŋŋɔpo̱ bre̱sɛ‑ɔ a ko̱so̱ ye̱re̱ bamo̱ pɛɛɛ akatɔ‑rɔ, ne̱ oo bise Yeesuu fe̱yɛ, “Amɔ fo̱ mo̱ŋ de ase̱ŋ mɔ pɛɛɛ ne̱ ba tɔwe̱ ba kye fo̱‑ɔ kanɔlee aaa?”
translated: Then Wuribware̱ asked them again, “Why are you arguing with each other on the way?”

ref: Some of the people started spitting on Jesus. They blindfolded him, hit him with their fists, and said, “Tell us who hit you!” Then the guards took charge of Jesus and beat him. Peter Says He Doesn't Know Jesus (Matthew
text: Mfe̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ bo̱ko̱ a le̱e̱ ba twe̱e̱ akyɔne̱ ba we̱e̱ Yeesuu si. Ne̱ baa ba kaprakɛɛ bo̱ buŋ mò̱ akatɔ se̱, na ba da mò̱ akuŋtiŋ. translated: One day, as he was teaching the people in the temple and preaching the good news, the chief priests and the teachers of the law, together with the elders, came up to him. ref: At once the rooster crowed a second time. Then Peter remembered that Jesus had told him, “Before a rooster crows twice, you will say three times that you don't know me.” So Peter started crying.
text: Mò̱ ke̱de̱ŋ tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ kyaŋare̱ a fo̱re̱ nyɔse̱po̱. Ne̱ oo nyiŋŋi ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱‑ɔ se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Fo̱ e̱ swe̱e̱ iluwi e̱sa fe̱yɛ fo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi mo̱ pwɛɛ na kyaŋare̱ dɛɛ fo̱re̱ iluwi e̱nyɔ.” Ne̱ ɔɔ ya su bwe̱e̱tɔ.
translated: When they brought the child to him, he placed his hands on the child and prayed. Then Jesus said to his followers, “Let the children come to me and do not stop them, for the kingdom of God belongs to such as these.” And he blessed them.

ref: But the chief priests told the crowd to ask Pilate to free Barabbas.
text: Amo̱ fe̱raa ne̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ a da lamaŋ‑ɔ kanɔ se̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ tɔwe̱ ɛ o lee Bar‑Abas. Ne̱ bo̱ yɛ, “Lee Bar‑Abas.”
translated: But the elders and the leaders persuaded the people to ask for Bar-Abas and to have Jesus killed. So they shouted, “Bar-Abas.”

translated: The woman said to her child, “Don’t go near the river bank!”
